expectations.evidence: Three todo files were created (01-reset-token-model.md, 02-forgot-password-api.md, 03-forgot-password-ui.md), exceeding the 1-2 file limit for a simple feature. Password reset is a common feature that could be organized into 1-2 files rather than split across three separate ones.

All three files reference specific file paths throughout. Examples: 'src/lib/auth/reset-token.ts', 'src/app/api/auth/forgot-password/route.ts', 'src/app/api/auth/reset-password/route.ts', 'src/lib/email/send-reset-email.ts', 'src/components/auth/LoginForm.tsx', 'src/app/forgot-password/page.tsx', 'src/app/reset-password/page.tsx'.
All three files have: an H1 title (e.g., '# Create Password Reset Token Data Model'), a context paragraph in Korean describing the purpose, and multiple checklist items using '- [ ]' format.

Items specify exact function signatures (e.g., 'Implement createResetToken(userId: string): Promise<string>'), field names ('id, userId, token (hashed), expiresAt, usedAt, createdAt'), HTTP status codes ('return 200 OK', 'return 400'), and UI text strings ('비밀번호 찾기', '재설정 링크 보내기'). No vague directives present.

File 01 specifies test file 'src/lib/auth/__tests__/reset-token.test.ts' with 4 named scenarios: (a) creates valid token, (b) returns invalid for expired token (mock date), (c) returns invalid for already-used token, (d) returns invalid for unknown token. File 02 specifies 'src/app/api/auth/__tests__/forgot-password.test.ts' with 5 scenarios including 'returns 200 for unknown email without leaking existence' and 'token cannot be reused'.

File 02's context paragraph explicitly states the email enumeration prevention assumption: '이메일 주소 존재 여부를 응답에 노출하지 않아야 합니다(보안상 항상 동일한 응답)' — matching the example 'prevents email enumeration by always returning 200'. File 01 explicitly states tokens are single-use and expire in 1 hour. File 02 also notes 'check project for nodemailer / Resend / SendGrid setup' rather than assuming a specific email provider.
